rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in the correct scope: type annexes for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Use subcomponent ports only in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections are defined
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the appropriate scope based on the requirement and AADL model structure
Preserve the existing AADL architecture by maintaining the correct scope and context for AGREE annex blocks
Use subcomponent ports only in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections are defined
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AGREE assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
